stories.headline: Denver DA Seeks Adult Charges for 12- and 14-Year-Olds in Sunnyside Murder Case
stories.summary:
Denver District Attorney John Walsh has filed a motion to transfer the cases of two children — aged 12 and 14 — to adult court in connection with the June 30 fatal shooting of 33-year-old Christopher Nabors near North Pecos Street and West 43rd Avenue. The children allegedly shot Nabors after he confronted them while they were attempting to break into or steal his car. Police arrested both juveniles the following day after a short chase in a stolen vehicle.

Detectives have since linked the 14-year-old and two other teens to a separate shooting near Park Avenue and East 20th Avenue roughly 15 minutes before the Nabors killing, in which a juvenile victim was injured but survived. Investigators are also examining whether the same two children are connected to a homicide that occurred June 24 in Denver's Montbello neighborhood.

The Denver Office of the Medical Examiner ruled Nabors' death a homicide caused by a gunshot wound. The DA's motion to try the children as adults marks a significant escalation in a case that has drawn attention to juvenile violent crime in Denver. If the transfer is granted, the children would face adult criminal proceedings.

stories.headline: Update: Denver Man Charged With Murder of Pregnant Wife; Affidavit Reveals Disturbing Details
stories.summary:
Herman Antonio Sisneros, 41, faces charges of first-degree murder and unlawful termination of a pregnancy after his 39-year-old wife, Angelica Sisneros, was found dead in the backyard of their South Xavier Street home Sunday. She was eight months pregnant at the time of her death. Sisneros called 911 but refused to explain the situation until officers arrived, and he initially blocked mental health clinicians from checking on his wife.

According to the arrest affidavit, Sisneros told clinicians he 'had to do what he did to protect the world' and that he was 'possibly brainwashed.' Multiple children younger than 10 were found in the home; they told officers their father had physically and verbally abused their mother, and one child said Sisneros struck Angelica in the stomach with a toy. Officers had responded to the home the previous day for a domestic disturbance.

Sisneros also faces four misdemeanor counts of negligent child abuse. He remained in custody at Denver's Downtown Detention Center as of Thursday and was scheduled for an advisement hearing Friday. The Denver Office of the Medical Examiner will determine the official cause and manner of Angelica Sisneros' death.

stories.headline: Arvada Mother in Daughter's Alcohol Death Denied Bond Reduction; Went to Rehab Days After Teen Died
stories.summary:
A Jefferson County judge on Thursday refused to reduce the $500,000 cash-only bond for Gretchen Ryan, 55, charged with second-degree murder in the death of her 16-year-old daughter Grace, who died March 9 in the family's Arvada home from chronic alcoholism. Ryan's defense attorneys argued she entered rehabilitation just seven days after Grace's death and has remained sober since police arrived at her home that day.

Prosecutors alleged Ryan supplied her daughter with significant amounts of alcohol — each consuming roughly a bottle of vodka per day — and concealed their drinking from Grace's father. Police found 173 empty alcohol bottles hidden in Grace's bedroom. The affidavit shows Grace repeatedly begged her mother for help, messaging her five days before her death: 'I'm in so much pain I can't keep doing this. Do something.'

Chief Deputy DA Tyra Forbes noted the bitter irony that Ryan eventually sought for herself the very rehab treatment her daughter had repeatedly requested. County Court Judge Sara Garrido said she was not basing the bond decision on the severity of the allegations, but concluded she had no confidence Ryan would return to court given the gravity of the charges she faces.

stories.headline: One Dead in Three-Vehicle Crash on Highway 119 East of Nederland
stories.summary:
A 37-year-old Nederland woman died Wednesday evening when a Dodge Ram with failed brakes lost control on Highway 119, sending its trailer into oncoming traffic and striking two vehicles. The driver of a Toyota Sienna was hospitalized with moderate injuries, while the Ram's driver was uninjured. The driver of the Ioniq struck by the trailer was pronounced dead at the scene.

Colorado State Patrol said the Ram was traveling eastbound when its brakes failed, causing the trailer to rotate into westbound lanes. The Sienna and Ioniq were pushed off the roadway upon impact. Highway 119 was closed for five miles between Nederland and Boulder for several hours before reopening around 4:15 a.m. Thursday.

The investigation remains ongoing. Witnesses or those with information are asked to contact CSP dispatch at 303-239-4501, referencing case number VC260232.

stories.headline: Polis-Progressive Rift Deepens as Governor Invokes Zimbabwe in Socialist Critique
stories.summary:
Colorado Gov. Jared Polis reignited an ideological feud within the state Democratic Party this week, posting a reference to Zimbabwe's failed socialist experiment in response to criticism from a left-leaning state legislator. Polis declared he does not want 'socialists' to 'destroy' his state, drawing a sharp rebuke from state Rep. Yara Zokaie (D).

Zokaie responded on social media, arguing that Polis, one of Colorado's wealthiest politicians, cannot simultaneously support universal health care while protecting insurance company profits. The exchange laid bare an ongoing tension between the governor's business-friendly, libertarian-leaning Democratic politics and the growing progressive wing of the party.

The dispute comes as Democratic Socialists of America candidates have made gains in other parts of the country, amplifying national conversations about the ideological direction of the Democratic Party. The Colorado confrontation reflects similar fault lines playing out in Democratic politics across the United States.

stories.headline: Denver Office Visits Still 39% Below Pre-Pandemic Levels Despite Year-Over-Year Gains
stories.summary:
A new report from location analytics firm Placer.ai shows Denver office visits in June remained 39 percent below 2019 levels, lagging behind other major U.S. cities even as the city posted year-over-year improvement. The findings have renewed scrutiny of downtown Denver's economic recovery and the pace of office reoccupancy in the metro area.

Denver Mayor Mike Johnston pushed back on the characterization that Denver has one of the emptiest downtowns among major American cities, disputing the framing of the data. The report nonetheless highlights the persistent challenge facing Denver's central business district, where office vacancy rates have remained elevated since the COVID-19 pandemic reshaped work patterns.

The data carries implications for downtown retail, transit ridership, and city tax revenues, all of which are sensitive to the volume of daily office workers. City officials and business groups have been working to attract return-to-office activity and new employers to bolster foot traffic in the urban core.

stories.headline: Denver Metro Launches $1,500 Heat Pump Rebate to Reduce Building Emissions
stories.summary:
The Denver Regional Council of Governments' Power Ahead Colorado program is offering $1,500 rebates for homeowners and businesses in the nine-county Denver metro region who install energy-efficient, cold-climate heat pumps. The initiative is part of a $40 million, four-year program backed by $200 million in federal funds, aimed at reducing air pollution from the building sector, which officials describe as the largest source of climate pollution in the Denver region.

Eligible households and businesses are located in Adams, Arapahoe, Boulder, Broomfield, Clear Creek, Denver, Douglas, Jefferson, Gilpin and a portion of Weld counties. The units must be Energy Star-rated and installed by a verified Power Ahead Colorado contractor. The rebate can be stacked with other incentives, including Xcel Energy rebates of up to $10,000 on an average system and a $1,000 state heat pump tax credit.

Power Ahead Colorado is also providing free energy advisers by phone and email to help residents navigate the installation process. Officials noted that the largest federal rebate program for heat pumps, the Home Electrification and Appliance Rebates program, closed to Front Range applicants in April, making the new regional rebate a timely alternative.

stories.headline: Colorado Wildfires Continue to Stress Front Range as Multiple Blazes Burn Statewide
stories.summary:
Several large wildfires continued burning across Colorado on Thursday, compounding the effects of an ongoing heat wave and historic drought. The Aspen Acres fire, the state's largest active blaze at nearly 100,000 acres near Rye in Custer and Pueblo counties, remained only 36 percent contained after destroying more than 775 structures and forcing evacuations of roughly 11,000 people. A team from Alaska has assumed command of the fire.

The Gold Mountain fire near Ouray has grown to more than 37,700 acres at 11 percent containment, and claimed the life of Canadian helicopter pilot Nicholas Dale, 56, when his aircraft crashed into Silver Jack Reservoir on July 12. Gov. Polis declared a disaster emergency for that fire. The Ferris fire in the San Juan National Forest, a merger of three lightning-caused blazes, has burned nearly 65,000 acres and is 53 percent contained.

A squirrel contacting a power line ignited a separate two-acre vegetation fire in Douglas County on Wednesday, briefly forcing the evacuation of five homes before firefighters contained it. The incident underscored the extreme fire danger across Colorado's drought-stricken Front Range, where smoke from multiple fires continued to degrade air quality even when not directly visible.

stories.headline: Hot, Dry Weather to Continue in Denver Through Weekend With Air Quality Concerns
stories.summary:
Denver faces another day of hot, dry weather Thursday with a forecast high near 93 degrees and variable winds up to 22 miles per hour, according to the National Weather Service. High pressure is blocking any moisture from reaching the region, and forecasters do not anticipate rainfall until at least early next week.

Smoke from multiple wildfires burning across Colorado is contributing to poor air quality across the metro area. While smoke may not be fully visible at ground level, health officials warn its effects will be present throughout the day.

The prolonged heat and drought are affecting recreational activities across the Front Range, including river tubing on Clear Creek in Golden and Boulder Creek, where record-low snowpack has resulted in below-normal water flows earlier in the season than usual. Outfitters and recreation officials say visitation has remained strong despite lower water levels.

stories.headline: City Park Adventure Hub Opens, Bringing Pedal Boats and Water Rentals Back to Denver
stories.summary:
Denver Parks and Recreation officially opened an Adventure Hub at City Park's Ferril Lake on Wednesday, offering residents pedal boats, kayaks, canoes, stand-up paddleboards and bicycles for rent. The hub is the second such facility to open this year, following a similar operation at Washington Park in early June. Rentals range from $15 per hour for a single kayak to $45 per hour for a six-person surrey bike.

The opening marks the first time in at least two decades that Denver has managed gear rentals in-house at City Park, after ending a long-running contract with vendor Wheel Fun Rentals earlier this year. City officials said bringing operations in-house is more cost-effective and gives the city greater control over the visitor experience. The move is part of a broader Outdoor Adventure and Alternative Sports Strategic Plan.

A third Adventure Hub is under construction at Ruby Hill and is expected to open later this year or in early 2027. The City Park hub is open daily from 10 a.m. to 9 p.m., while the Washington Park location operates from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m., both closed on city-observed holidays.

stories.headline: Low Snowpack Cuts Short Creek Tubing Season Along Denver's Front Range
stories.summary:
Record-low snowpack from an exceptionally dry winter is reducing water flows in Clear Creek, Boulder Creek and other Front Range waterways, leading to an earlier and shorter tubing and river recreation season than usual. Golden's Parks and Recreation director said visitation at Clear Creek remains strong despite lower-than-normal water levels, driven by the persistent heat wave gripping the metro area.

In Englewood, the wave feature at River Run park is currently not surfable due to insufficient flow, and it remains unclear whether conditions will improve later in the summer. Boulder Creek saw significantly lower-than-normal spring runoff this year, tapering off more quickly than usual, according to a local outfitter.

Despite the challenges, some operators say lower water levels have actually improved the tubing experience at certain locations by creating calmer, less intense conditions. At Clear Creek in Golden, Adventure West began renting tubes in late May — a month earlier than normal — to capture the limited season. Outfitters and city recreation officials across the region are adjusting operations to account for what may be an abbreviated water recreation window in 2026.

stories.headline: Broncos' Offensive Line Preview: Veteran Group Heads Into Training Camp With High Expectations
stories.summary:
The Denver Broncos enter the 2026 season with one of the NFL's most experienced and expensive offensive lines, featuring returning first-team All-Pros Quinn Meinerz and Garett Bolles alongside Mike McGlinchey, Ben Powers and Luke Wattenberg. The unit has combined for 321 starts as Broncos and 426 total NFL starts, and its members are collectively among the top-paid at their positions leaguewide.

The most impactful offseason move for the group was re-signing versatile lineman Alex Palczewski to a two-year, $9.5 million deal. Palczewski started 10 games in 2025 and filled in at multiple positions — including left guard — when Powers tore his bicep. Denver also selected Kage Casey in the fourth round of April's draft to add developmental depth.

The primary question heading into training camp, which will take place at the Broncos' new facility, is whether the starting five can remain healthy through the season. Bolles (34), McGlinchey (32) and Powers (29) will all be 30 or older by the team's bye week, introducing age-related durability risk to what is otherwise considered an elite unit. Offensive coordinator Webb's play-calling could determine whether Denver can establish a stronger ground game to complement what has been an elite pass-protecting front.

weather.notable: Nearly 5 degrees above the average high for this time of year.

joke.joke: My wife and I have been married for so long we finish each other's... we finish each other's...  See, she's not here, so I can't finish.

weekendEvents.title: Maren Morris with The Highwomen & Brittney Spencer
weekendEvents.description: Grammy-winning country-pop artist Maren Morris headlines Red Rocks Amphitheatre alongside The Highwomen and Brittney Spencer in one of the weekend's marquee outdoor concerts.

weekendEvents.title: The String Cheese Incident at Red Rocks
weekendEvents.description: Colorado jam band The String Cheese Incident continues their annual Red Rocks tradition with improvisational rock, bluegrass, and extended live performances.

weekendEvents.title: Tyler Childers at Fiddler's Green
weekendEvents.description: Country artist Tyler Childers brings his Appalachian-inspired songwriting to Fiddler's Green Amphitheatre for an evening of heartfelt country music.

weekendEvents.title: Paul Oakenfold & The Crystal Method at Cervantes'
weekendEvents.description: Electronic music legends Paul Oakenfold and The Crystal Method headline a dance party featuring trance, breakbeat, and big-room classics at Cervantes' Masterpiece Ballroom.
weekendEvents.location: Cervantes' Masterpiece Ballroom, 2637 Welton St, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Marcos Valle at Bluebird Theater
weekendEvents.description: Brazilian music icon Marcos Valle performs a blend of jazz, samba, bossa nova, and funk at the Bluebird Theater.
weekendEvents.location: Bluebird Theater, 3317 E Colfax Ave, Denver

weekendEvents.title: Global Climbing Day at Movement RiNo
weekendEvents.description: Movement RiNo and The North Face celebrate Global Climbing Day with free gym access all day, complimentary gear rentals, yoga, special programming, and appearances by professional climbers Andres Marin and Cedar Wright.

weekendEvents.title: Zootopia 2 Screening with Denver Zoo Animal Ambassadors
weekendEvents.description: The Historic Elitch Theatre hosts a free outdoor screening of Zootopia 2, preceded by a live animal ambassador program featuring animals from the Denver Zoo Conservation Alliance.
weekendEvents.time: Doors 6 p.m., animal program 6:45 p.m., film 7:30 p.m.
weekendEvents.location: Historic Elitch Theatre, 4600 W. 37th Place, Denver

weekendEvents.title: The Great Bubble Fest + Market
weekendEvents.description: A free family-friendly outdoor event at Aspen Grove Shopping Center featuring bubble art, live music, food trucks, and a maker's market.
weekendEvents.location: Aspen Grove Shopping Center, 7301 S. Santa Fe Drive, Littleton

weekendEvents.title: Tennyson Street Fair
weekendEvents.description: An annual neighborhood street fair along Denver's Tennyson Street corridor featuring local vendors, food, and entertainment.

weekendEvents.title: Lavender Festival at Chatfield Farms
weekendEvents.description: Denver Botanic Gardens' Chatfield Farms hosts its annual Lavender Festival with lavender fields in bloom, vendors, food, and educational programming.

weekendEvents.title: Drawing Club at BMoCA
weekendEvents.description: The Boulder Museum of Contemporary Art hosts an open drop-in drawing session with music and light snacks provided — sketches, paints, and all skill levels welcome. No registration required.
weekendEvents.location: Boulder Museum of Contemporary Art, 1750 13th St., Boulder
